title: [WIP] [skip ci] Cirrus: Simplify task execution conditions
body:
***---DRAFT---INCOMPLETE---LIKELY BROKEN---***

There are a great number of CI tasks and execution conditions, all crammed
into a single YAML file.  There are also two distinct methods for
allowing or blocking tasks from executing.  Combined, these factors make
it very difficult for humans to see/understand the big picture of "what
runs, and when".  Fix this by:

* Adding documentation
* Remove all use of the `skip` keyword to simplify logic
* Create simple definitions for each conditional element
* Re-write all `only_if` definitions using simplified elements

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich* To complete the [pull request process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process), please assign **ashley-cui** after the PR has been reviewed. You can assign the PR to them by writing `/assign @ashley-cui` in a comment when ready. The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@edsantiago when you have a moment, could you take a quick "big-picture" look at this?  It's thrown together, unverified, likely incorrect, but with some spit/polish do you think it could be an improvement?

Example Builds (useful to see all the `CIRRUS_*` variables):
* [Pull Request invocation](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/4780330455400448?logs=setup#L4)
* [Branch invocation](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6582594966913024?logs=setup#L4)
* [Tag invocation](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/4581520814899200?logs=setup#L4)
* [Cirrus-Cron invocation](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6354054824591360?logs=setup#L4)

@cevich looks good in principle but some of the clauses are hard to read (`only_if: '!($IS_DOCS || $IS_MA)'` ?!?) and some are repeated enough times that they could be refactored into a legible var name (`$IS_PR && !($IS_DOCS || $IS_BUILD || $IS_MA)'` => `$IS_CODE_PR`)

I'll look again when the CI failure is fixed.

> @cevich looks good in principle but some of the clauses are hard to read

Thanks for the feedback, ya I'm sure there are lots of tweaks needed.  It doesn't really remove much of the complexity unfortunately, but hopefully we can make it a little more readable.

> I'll look again when the CI failure is fixed.

That failure worries me actually.  It seems to suggest my trick of using env. vars. might not work in the `only_if` context.

@edsantiago ya, using env. vars. in `only_if` or `skip` won't work :cry: I remember I asked Cirrus-CI to fix this, they did, and it broke all kinds of other things.  Plan B:

```YAML
env:
    ...
    # N/B: The following env. vars. are only used to define YAML-anchors
    _pr_mode: &_pr_mode <insert logic here>
    _docs_mode: &_docs_mode <insert logic here>
    _branch_mode: ...stuff...
    _tag_mode: ...things...
    ...

some_task:
    only_if: *_pr_mode
    ...
```

That would work, but @edsantiago would it improve readability/maintainability?

I'm having trouble understanding the logic of your suggestion; but IIUC it couldn't work for multiple conditions (ANDs and ORs), so it doesn't seem worth it. But TBH I no longer even read those conditions, I use svg mode in `cirrus-task-map`, which lets me hover mouse over conditions to ghost them out.

> IIUC it couldn't work for multiple conditions (ANDs and ORs),

Yeah, I'd need to spell every condition out fully.  Really the only advantage would be to collect/organize all the conditions together in a single place at the top of the YAML.  Hmmm.  Yeah, it's probably not worth tinkering with.
